title: Electric powered hydrofoil watercraft with multiple batteries
description: Board-style electric hydrofoil filing covering multiple/removable battery-pack architecture, mast/foil integration, and battery placement concepts to extend range while managing stability.
relevance: LOW — Appears oriented to single-rider board/eFoil form factors rather than 2-person seated craft. Still useful prior art for battery modularity concepts.

title: Watercraft Having a Lift Assembly (BRP/Sea-Doo)
assignee: Bombardier Recreational Products Inc. (BRP) / BRP US Inc.
description: Hydrofoil personal watercraft patent covering a straddle-seat hull with mast/foil lift assembly, pump-jet propulsion, movable foil surfaces (elevons), and control logic that links handlebar position to foil pitch (manual + sensor-assisted automated modes). Claims include retractable/movable hydrofoil arrangements and control-unit-managed pitch/height stabilization.
relevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Adjacent IP from the largest global PWC OEM. Not a direct 2-person couch craft today, but highly relevant for hydrofoil PWC control architecture and future multi-passenger product expansion.

title: Neocean Overboat Inverted-T Foil Flight Control System (acquired by SeaBubbles)
assignee: SeaBubbles (acquired from Neocean / Electro Nautic France)
description: Covers flight control systems for inverted-T hydrofoil watercraft, developed for the Overboat catamaran. SeaBubbles acquired these patents in Sept 2024 to accelerate their SmartBubble foil control development. The inverted-T foil with active stabilization (100Hz control loop) is the core claimed IP.
relevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Kai Concepts must audit these claims before finalizing foil design. Inverted-T is a common foil geometry. SeaBubbles now controls key flight control IP in this space.

title: WaveDrive Propulsion System (ENAUTIC)
description: Core propulsion and foil control technology powering WaveFlyer Volaré. Enables autonomous stabilization and efficient hydrofoiling. Claims cover the integrated drive-and-foil-control approach.
relevance: HIGH — direct competitor tech. ENAUTIC's core IP. Defines what Kai Concepts cannot copy.

title: Hydrofoil Boat with Counter-Rotating Motor Pod (Candela)
description: Covers a boat with two hydrofoils where the rear strut carries a motor pod with counter-rotating propellers, which is turnable for steering. This is essentially the C-Pod system architecture.
relevance: MEDIUM — Candela's core drivetrain patent. Relevant if Kai Concepts uses similar pod-on-strut approach.

title: Multi-Purpose Electric Hydrofoil
description: A hydrofoil assembly adapted to be removably attached to a watercraft. Covers modular hydrofoil attachment systems. Published 2023.
relevance: LOW — broad modular attachment claim. Monitor for grant.

title: Hydrofoil Vessel (Norwegian PCT)
description: New WIPO PCT application from Norway for a hydrofoil vessel design. Filed October 2024. Details limited as still pending publication.
relevance: LOW — monitor for claims as it publishes.

title: Watercraft with Hydrofoil (SubSeaSail)
description: A watercraft including a board with top/bottom surface and a hydrofoil attached to a strut. Covers hydrofoil board attachment geometries.
relevance: LOW — covers board-style craft, not seated platforms. Monitor for reissue/continuation.

title: Lift-Propulsion System for a Watercraft and Watercraft Having Same (BRP/Sea-Doo)
description: Patent covers a hydrofoil watercraft with integrated lift-propulsion system, depicted as a surfboard-shaped hull with foil below. Appears to be an eFoil-category design. Part of BRP/Sea-Doo's push into electric powersports.
relevance: MEDIUM — BRP is the largest PWC OEM globally. If they commercialize this, it signals full mainstream legitimacy for electric hydrofoiling and creates a mass-market reference point. Unlikely to threaten Kai Concepts' couch niche directly, but watch for subsequent patents covering larger or multi-person configurations.

title: Electric Watercraft Design (UNI Electric Watercraft Group)
description: Design patent for an electric watercraft — covers ornamental design.
relevance: LOW — design patent, doesn't block functional innovation.
